name: youthub
description: YouHub — console YouTube client. TUI grid, custom ffplay-yt player, SABR streaming, SponsorBlock, 1080p.
readme_summary: YouHub is a terminal-based YouTube client that allows users to browse, search, and watch YouTube videos directly from their console. It features a TUI grid interface, a custom ffplay-yt player, and integrates features like SponsorBlock for an ad-free experience without needing a web browser.
primary_use_case: Watching YouTube videos directly from the terminal without using a web browser.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, retro-futuristic terminal window displaying a vibrant grid of YouTube video thumbnails. In the center, a user's hand is shown interacting with a glowing cursor, selecting a video. From the selected thumbnail, a stream of digital particles flows into a minimalist video player interface that appears to be projected from the terminal itself. Subtle elements like a small SponsorBlock icon and a progress bar indicate advanced features. The overall mood is one of seamless integration and efficient content consumption within a command-line environment. The art style should be a clean, 3D isometric illustration with a dark, tech-inspired color palette.

name: DeadReckoning
description: Real-time localization on Android phones using inertial sensors (accelerometer, compass, gyro)
readme_summary: Dead Reckoning is an Android application that provides real-time localization using a phone's inertial sensors, eliminating the need for GPS. It enables self-sufficient and offline location tracking, useful in environments where GPS signals are unreliable or unavailable.
primary_use_case: To provide accurate and reliable location tracking on Android devices, especially in environments where GPS is not available or functional.
cover_image_prompt: A lone hiker confidently navigating a dense, misty forest, holding up a smartphone. The phone screen displays a glowing, precise path overlaying a simplified map of the forest, with no visible satellite signals. Subtle digital particles emanate from the phone, tracing the path through the trees. In the background, a faint, broken GPS signal icon is visible, emphasizing its unreliability. The scene is rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a focus on clarity and a slightly adventurous, self-reliant mood. The repository name 'DeadReckoning' is subtly integrated into a small, stylized compass on the phone's interface.

name: branch-output
description: This is an OBS Studio plugin that allows to transmit video and audio with RTMP / SRT etc. for each source individually.
readme_summary: Branch Output is an OBS Studio plugin that enhances streaming and recording capabilities by allowing individual sources or scenes to be configured with dedicated encoding settings and audio sources. It provides advanced features like automatic reconnection, per-filter replay buffers, and manual file splitting, offering granular control over output streams.
key_features: Individual streaming and recording per source/scene
Stream recording with container format and division support
Status dock for monitoring and control
Manual file splitting, pause/unpause recording, chapter markers
Video cropping and frame rate division
Proc handler for save file name format override
primary_use_case: To provide advanced, multi-output streaming and recording capabilities for OBS Studio, allowing users to send individual sources or scenes to different destinations with custom configurations.
cover_image_prompt: A conductor standing at a podium, orchestrating multiple distinct streams of light emanating from different musical instruments (representing OBS sources). Each stream of light is a different color and thickness, flowing into separate, stylized broadcasting towers. The conductor's baton glows, subtly showing UI elements of encoding settings. Small, floating digital particles and subtle 'Branch Output' text integrated into the scene connect it to software. The overall mood is dynamic and controlled, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with vibrant colors and clear visual separation of elements.

name: Claude-Code-Game-Studios
description: Turn Claude Code into a full game dev studio — 49 AI agents, 72 workflow skills, and a complete coordination system mirroring real studio hierarchy.
readme_summary: Claude Code Game Studios transforms a single Claude Code session into a structured, multi-agent game development studio. It organizes AI agents into a hierarchy with specialized roles and skills, mirroring a real studio's workflow to improve project organization, catch errors early, and maintain project vision.
key_features: 49 specialized AI agents organized into a studio hierarchy (Directors, Department Leads, Specialists)
73 workflow skills (slash commands) for all phases of game development
12 automated hooks for validation on commits, pushes, asset changes, and more
11 path-scoped rules for enforcing coding standards
41 document templates for GDDs, UX specs, ADRs, sprint plans, etc.
Mimics real studio hierarchy for structured AI-assisted development
primary_use_case: To provide structure and organization to AI-assisted game development within a single Claude Code session, enabling a more efficient and professional workflow.
cover_image_prompt: A conductor leading a symphony orchestra, where each musician is an AI agent represented by a stylized Claude chatbot. The conductor, holding a glowing baton, directs the flow of musical notes (representing code and game assets) that are being meticulously composed and arranged into a grand, cohesive game world visualized on a large holographic screen in the background. Subtle UI elements showing code snippets and design documents are integrated into the scene. The overall mood is collaborative and creative, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The art style is 3D isometric illustration with vibrant colors and clear outlines, emphasizing the organized chaos and harmonious creation of a game.

name: GenericAgent
description: Self-evolving agent: grows skill tree from 3.3K-line seed, achieving full system control with 6x less token consumption
readme_summary: GenericAgent is a Python framework for creating self-evolving autonomous AI agents. It allows LLMs to control local computer systems through a minimal set of tools and an agent loop, automatically learning and growing its capabilities into a personal skill tree with each task it completes.
key_features: Self-Evolving: Automatically crystallizes tasks into reusable Skills, growing a personal skill tree.
Minimal Architecture: Core code is ~3K lines, Agent Loop is ~100 lines, with no complex dependencies.
Strong Execution: Injects into real browsers and uses 9 atomic tools for direct system control.
High Compatibility: Supports major LLMs (Claude, Gemini, etc.) and is cross-platform.
Token Efficient: Operates within a <30K context window, reducing cost and hallucinations.
primary_use_case: Enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to autonomously control and interact with local computer systems, automating tasks and evolving capabilities over time.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, ancient tree with glowing digital roots, representing the 'GenericAgent' skill tree. Each branch sprouts a unique, intricate tool icon (like a browser window, a terminal, a mouse cursor, a file folder). A small, stylized AI entity (representing an LLM) is shown carefully tending to the tree, pruning old branches and nurturing new growth, symbolizing self-evolution. Subtle streams of data flow from the AI to the tree, and from the tree to a simplified representation of a computer desktop in the background. The overall mood is one of organic growth and intelligent automation, rendered in a vibrant, digital painting style with a focus on clear iconography and a clean composition.
